(Russian: Юрий Юрьевич Ларионов, born 19 August 1986) is a Russian [pair skater](/wiki/Pair_skater "Pair skater"). With former partner [Vera Bazarova](/wiki/Vera_Bazarova "Vera Bazarova"), he is the [2012–13 Grand Prix Final](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating_Final "2012–13 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final") silver medalist, a three-time [European](/wiki/European_Figure_Skating_Championships "European Figure Skating Championships") medalist (silver in [2012](/wiki/2012_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2012 European Figure Skating Championships"); bronze in [2011](/wiki/2011_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2011 European Figure Skating Championships") and [2014](/wiki/2014_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2014 European Figure Skating Championships")), the [2007 World Junior](/wiki/2007_World_Junior_Figure_Skating_Championships "2007 World Junior Figure Skating Championships") silver medalist, and the [2012 Russian national](/wiki/2012_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2012 Russian Figure Skating Championships") champion. They won six senior [Grand Prix series](/wiki/ISU_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating "ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating") medals outside the final, including gold at the [2012 NHK Trophy](/wiki/2012_NHK_Trophy "2012 NHK Trophy").

**Yuri Yuryevich Larionov** (Russian: Юрий Юрьевич Ларионов, born 19 August 1986) is a Russian [pair skater](/wiki/Pair_skater "Pair skater"). With former partner [Vera Bazarova](/wiki/Vera_Bazarova "Vera Bazarova"), he is the [2012–13 Grand Prix Final](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating_Final "2012–13 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final") silver medalist, a three-time [European](/wiki/European_Figure_Skating_Championships "European Figure Skating Championships") medalist (silver in [2012](/wiki/2012_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2012 European Figure Skating Championships"); bronze in [2011](/wiki/2011_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2011 European Figure Skating Championships") and [2014](/wiki/2014_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2014 European Figure Skating Championships")), the [2007 World Junior](/wiki/2007_World_Junior_Figure_Skating_Championships "2007 World Junior Figure Skating Championships") silver medalist, and the [2012 Russian national](/wiki/2012_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2012 Russian Figure Skating Championships") champion. They won six senior [Grand Prix series](/wiki/ISU_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating "ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating") medals outside the final, including gold at the [2012 NHK Trophy](/wiki/2012_NHK_Trophy "2012 NHK Trophy").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Larionov was born on 19 August 1986 in [Novosibirsk](/wiki/Novosibirsk "Novosibirsk"), [Russian SFSR](/wiki/Russian_SFSR "Russian SFSR"), Soviet Union.[[1]](#cite_note-ISU-1314-1) His family later moved to [Tashkent](/wiki/Tashkent "Tashkent"), [Uzbek SSR](/wiki/Uzbek_SSR "Uzbek SSR"), due to his father's army service.[[2]](#cite_note-GS100704-2) He began skating in 1990,[[1]](#cite_note-ISU-1314-1) introduced to the activity by his mother, who was concerned about his health.[[2]](#cite_note-GS100704-2) He decided to continue skating after the family returned to Novosibirsk and later trained for four years in Moscow before moving to [Perm](/wiki/Perm,_Russia "Perm, Russia").[[2]](#cite_note-GS100704-2) His first skating partner was Dinara Kalimulina.[[1]](#cite_note-ISU-1314-1)

Larionov studied at Perm State Pedagogical University, Faculty of Physical Education.[[2]](#cite_note-GS100704-2)[[3]](#cite_note-permbreak-3)

## Partnership with Bazarova

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Partnership with Bazarova")]

### 2005–09 season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 2005–09 season")]

In spring 2005, coach [Ludmila Kalinina](/wiki/Ludmila_Kalinina "Ludmila Kalinina") paired him with [Vera Bazarova](/wiki/Vera_Bazarova "Vera Bazarova").[[2]](#cite_note-GS100704-2) They trained in Perm at the Orlenok Ice Palace until 2011.[[2]](#cite_note-GS100704-2)[[4]](#cite_note-RG110627-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6e/Liudmila_Kalinina_and_Vera_BAZAROVA_and_Yuri_LARIONOV.jpg/250px-Liudmila_Kalinina_and_Vera_BAZAROVA_and_Yuri_LARIONOV.jpg)](/wiki/File:Liudmila_Kalinina_and_Vera_BAZAROVA_and_Yuri_LARIONOV.jpg)

Bazarova/Larionov at the [2007 Skate America](/wiki/2007_Skate_America "2007 Skate America") with former coach Ludmila Kalinina

Bazarova/Larionov took silver at the [2007 World Junior Championships](/wiki/2007_World_Junior_Figure_Skating_Championships "2007 World Junior Figure Skating Championships"). In October, they won their first senior international medal when they claimed the bronze at [2007 Skate America](/wiki/2007_Skate_America "2007 Skate America"). In December of the same year, the pair won gold at the [2007 Junior Grand Prix Final](/wiki/2007%E2%80%9308_ISU_Junior_Grand_Prix "2007–08 ISU Junior Grand Prix"). However, in January 2008, they were disqualified and stripped of the medal due to a [doping](/wiki/Doping_(sport) "Doping (sport)") violation found in a blood sample Larionov gave before the Final.[[5]](#cite_note-disqualified-5)[[6]](#cite_note-ban1-6)[[7]](#cite_note-IFS110612-7) Larionov was also suspended for two years, beginning on 18 January 2008.[[8]](#cite_note-ban2-8) His sentence was later reduced to 18 months and ended on 17 July 2009.[[9]](#cite_note-ban3-9) Bazarova decided to wait for Larionov: "There were offers from others, but I said right away that I'm not going to skate with someone else."[[2]](#cite_note-GS100704-2)

### 2009–10 season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: 2009–10 season")]

Bazarova/Larionov made their return to competition during the [2009–10 season](/wiki/2009%E2%80%9310_figure_skating_season "2009–10 figure skating season"). Their first event was the [2009 Cup of Russia](/wiki/2009_Cup_of_Russia "2009 Cup of Russia") where they placed fourth and they won the bronze medal at Russian Nationals, earning them further assignments. In January 2010, the pair finished 5th at the [European Championships](/wiki/2010_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2010 European Figure Skating Championships"). In February, they placed 11th at the Olympics and then completed their season with an 8th-place finish at the [World Championships](/wiki/2010_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2010 World Figure Skating Championships") in March. They were named Breakthrough of the Year (pair skating) at the 2010 Crystal Ice Awards held in October 2010 in Moscow.[[10]](#cite_note-AS101119-10)

### 2010–11 season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: 2010–11 season")]

Bazarova/Larionov began the [2010–11 season](/wiki/2010%E2%80%9311_figure_skating_season "2010–11 figure skating season") with a win at [2010 Nebelhorn Trophy](/wiki/2010_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2010 Nebelhorn Trophy") in September. Their first Grand Prix event was the [2010 NHK Trophy](/wiki/2010_NHK_Trophy "2010 NHK Trophy") in October, where they won the silver medal. At [2010 Trophée Eric Bompard](/wiki/2010_Troph%C3%A9e_Eric_Bompard "2010 Trophée Eric Bompard"), they won another silver medal, in doing so setting new personal best scores in the short program (64.18) and long program (118.82). Their results qualified them for the [Grand Prix Final](/wiki/2010%E2%80%9311_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating_Final "2010–11 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final"), their first at the senior level, where they finished fifth. At the [Russian Nationals](/wiki/2011_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2011 Russian Figure Skating Championships") they won their second bronze medal.

At the [2011 European Championships](/wiki/2011_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2011 European Figure Skating Championships"), they won the bronze medal after finishing third in both programs and setting personal best scores in the long program and combined total (188.24).[[11]](#cite_note-euro11pairssp-11)[[12]](#cite_note-euro11pairslp-12) Although many skaters, including those based at other Russian training centers, criticized the freezing conditions at the arena, Bazarova said, "At home in our ice rink in Perm, it can also be very cold so we are kind of used to it."[[12]](#cite_note-euro11pairslp-12) At the [2011 World Championships](/wiki/2011_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2011 World Figure Skating Championships"), they finished 5th.

### 2011–12 season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: 2011–12 season")]

In June 2011, it was reported that Bazarova/Larionov would move along with their coach, Ludmila Kalinina, from Perm to [Saransk](/wiki/Saransk "Saransk").[[4]](#cite_note-RG110627-4)[[13]](#cite_note-AIF110624-13) Bazarova said they were comfortable in Saransk, also the host of the 2011 and 2012 [Russian national championships](/wiki/Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "Russian Figure Skating Championships").[[14]](#cite_note-IN111128-14) The pair's first event of the [2011–12 season](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_figure_skating_season "2011–12 figure skating season") was [Nebelhorn Trophy](/wiki/2011_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2011 Nebelhorn Trophy"), where they won silver. They were 5th at [2011 Skate America](/wiki/2011_Skate_America "2011 Skate America"), their first [Grand Prix](/wiki/2011%E2%80%9312_ISU_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating "2011–12 ISU Grand Prix of Figure Skating") event of the season. At [2011 Trophée Eric Bompard](/wiki/2011_Troph%C3%A9e_Eric_Bompard "2011 Trophée Eric Bompard"), they won the silver medal with a new personal best in the free program. Bazarova/Larionov won their first national title at the [2012 Russian Championships](/wiki/2012_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2012 Russian Figure Skating Championships"). At the [2012 European Championships](/wiki/2012_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2012 European Figure Skating Championships"), they set new personal bests in their short and long programs. They scored a total of 193.79 points overall and won the silver medal. They finished 6th at the [2012 World Championships](/wiki/2012_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2012 World Figure Skating Championships"). Bazarova/Larionov were assigned to represent Team Russia at the [2012 World Team Trophy](/wiki/2012_ISU_World_Team_Trophy_in_Figure_Skating "2012 ISU World Team Trophy in Figure Skating"). They placed first in the pairs event.

### 2012–13 season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: 2012–13 season")]

Bazarova/Larionov decided to spend more time in Novogorsk (near Moscow) working with former Bolshoi soloist Liudmila Vlasova, who assisted them with interpretation.[[15]](#cite_note-gsGPF12PF-15)[[16]](#cite_note-SE121207-16) They withdrew from the [2012 Nebelhorn Trophy](/wiki/2012_Nebelhorn_Trophy "2012 Nebelhorn Trophy") after the short program due to a recurring injury to Bazarova's right hip.[[17]](#cite_note-GS120930-17)[[18]](#cite_note-FCP121002-18) On November 1, Larionov said the injury did not require an operation.[[19]](#cite_note-TR121101-19) Bazarova said it was not painful but her leg would feel very weak at times and that massage treated the problem effectively.[[16]](#cite_note-SE121207-16)

After taking silver at the [2012 Rostelecom Cup](/wiki/2012_Rostelecom_Cup "2012 Rostelecom Cup"), Bazarova/Larionov won their first Grand Prix title at the [2012 NHK Trophy](/wiki/2012_NHK_Trophy "2012 NHK Trophy").[[20]](#cite_note-gsNHK12PF-20)[[21]](#cite_note-gsNHK12PS-21) They qualified for their second [Grand Prix Final](/wiki/2012%E2%80%932013_Grand_Prix_of_Figure_Skating_Final "2012–2013 Grand Prix of Figure Skating Final"). At the final in [Sochi](/wiki/Sochi "Sochi"), Russia, they won the silver medal with personal best scores in both programs and a total of 201.60 points.

Bazarova/Larionov withdrew from the [2013 Russian Championships](/wiki/2013_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2013 Russian Figure Skating Championships"). [Aleksandr Gorshkov](/wiki/Aleksandr_Gorshkov_(figure_skater) "Aleksandr Gorshkov (figure skater)") said that Larionov had boot problems and could not endanger his partner.[[22]](#cite_note-RSP121224-22) The pair was named in the Russian team to the [2013 European Championships](/wiki/2013_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2013 European Figure Skating Championships") but withdrew due to Larionov's wrist injury which prevented him from doing a number of pairs elements.[[23]](#cite_note-RSP130118-23)[[24]](#cite_note-FSFR130202-24) They were replaced by [Ksenia Stolbova](/wiki/Ksenia_Stolbova "Ksenia Stolbova") / [Fedor Klimov](/wiki/Fedor_Klimov "Fedor Klimov"). Larionov had an operation in Moscow.[[24]](#cite_note-FSFR130202-24) On 21 February 2013, Larionov confirmed they were no longer working with [Ludmila Kalinina](/wiki/Ludmila_Kalinina "Ludmila Kalinina").[[25]](#cite_note-FCP130221-25) The pair trained in Novogorsk, Moscow, advised by [Viktor Kudriavtsev](/wiki/Viktor_Kudriavtsev "Viktor Kudriavtsev"), in the period leading up to the [2013 World Championships](/wiki/2013_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2013 World Figure Skating Championships"), with a decision on a new main coach likely to be made after the event.[[25]](#cite_note-FCP130221-25) Bazarova injured her hip on a fall while training in Detroit before Worlds.[[26]](#cite_note-TR130312-26) They finished 7th at the event.

On 26 March 2013, Bazarova/Larionov announced they would be coached by [Nina Mozer](/wiki/Nina_Mozer "Nina Mozer").[[27]](#cite_note-FSFR130326-27)[[28]](#cite_note-TR130326VB-28)[[29]](#cite_note-TR130326YL-29) In preparation for the 2013–14 season, the pair began training in Moscow and then travelled with Mozer's group to [West Orange, New Jersey](/wiki/West_Orange,_New_Jersey "West Orange, New Jersey") in May.[[30]](#cite_note-SE130413-30)[[31]](#cite_note-SE140327-31)

### 2013–14 season

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2013–14 season")]

At the start of the season, Bazarova/Larionov considered adding a quadruple twist but decided against it.[[32]](#cite_note-AS140327-32) During the 2013–14 Grand Prix series, they placed fourth at the [2013 Trophee Eric Bompard](/wiki/2013_Trophee_Eric_Bompard "2013 Trophee Eric Bompard") and then won the silver medal at the [2013 Rostelecom Cup](/wiki/2013_Rostelecom_Cup "2013 Rostelecom Cup"), behind [Aliona Savchenko](/wiki/Aliona_Savchenko "Aliona Savchenko") / [Robin Szolkowy](/wiki/Robin_Szolkowy "Robin Szolkowy"). After winning the [2013 NRW Trophy](/wiki/2013_NRW_Trophy "2013 NRW Trophy"), the pair took silver at the [2014 Russian Championships](/wiki/2014_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2014 Russian Figure Skating Championships"), losing by a margin of 0.45 to [Ksenia Stolbova](/wiki/Ksenia_Stolbova "Ksenia Stolbova") / [Fedor Klimov](/wiki/Fedor_Klimov "Fedor Klimov"). At the [2014 European Championships](/wiki/2014_European_Figure_Skating_Championships "2014 European Figure Skating Championships"), Bazarova/Larionov won the bronze medal, again finishing behind Stolbova/Klimov.

In February 2014, Bazarova/Larionov appeared at their second Olympics, in [Sochi](/wiki/Sochi "Sochi"). They were not selected for the [team event](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Team_trophy "Figure skating at the 2014 Winter Olympics – Team trophy") but did compete in the separate [pairs event](/wiki/Figure_skating_at_the_2014_Winter_Olympics_%E2%80%93_Pairs_skating "Figure skating at the 2014 Winter Olympics – Pairs skating"), placing sixth. The next month, they competed at the [2014 World Championships](/wiki/2014_World_Figure_Skating_Championships "2014 World Figure Skating Championships") in [Saitama](/wiki/Saitama,_Saitama "Saitama, Saitama"), Japan. During the free skate, Larionov stumbled while setting Bazarova down from a lift but they avoided injury and finished seventh overall. Later that day, 27 March 2014, he told Bazarova he wanted to end their partnership.[[33]](#cite_note-SS140401-33) Nina Mozer made the split public several days later and said she was working to arrange a new partnership for him.[[34]](#cite_note-IT140331-34)

## Partnership with Zabiyako

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Yuri_Larionov&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Partnership with Zabiyako")]

Larionov teamed up with Estonia's [Natalya Zabiyako](/wiki/Natalya_Zabiyako "Natalya Zabiyako") to compete for Russia.[[35]](#cite_note-fskate-35) At the [2015 Russian Championships](/wiki/2015_Russian_Figure_Skating_Championships "2015 Russian Figure Skating Championships"), they placed 5th in the short program and 7th overall. In June 2015, Zabiyako said that she and Larionov had ended their partnership.
